votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq logic, including the custom word splitting and consonant counting behavior.

The Python code correctly implements the logic of the Coq specification, including the vowel check, the consonant counting (which includes all non-vowel characters), the word splitting logic (handling multiple spaces and trailing spaces), and the final filtering based on the consonant count.
The Python code accurately implements the Coq definitions, including the exact logic for counting non-vowels and splitting strings by spaces.

The Python code correctly implements all Coq definitions: is_vowel checks the same characters, count_consonants counts non-vowels (including spaces, punctuation, etc.) the same way, split_words_aux splits on spaces identically, get_words calls split_words_aux with empty accumulator, and select_words_spec filters words by consonant count matching n. The iterative Python implementation of split_words_aux produces the same results as the recursive Coq version for all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements all the Coq definitions: is_vowel checks the same 10 vowel characters, count_consonants counts non-vowels (including spaces, punctuation, etc.), split_words_aux splits on spaces with the same accumulator logic, get_words calls split_words_aux with empty string, and select_words_spec filters words by consonant count matching n. The iterative Python implementation of split_words_aux produces the same results as the recursive Coq version for all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements all Coq definitions: is_vowel checks the same 10 vowel characters, count_consonants counts non-vowels (including spaces, punctuation, etc.) the same way, split_words_aux splits on spaces identically (accumulating non-space characters and emitting words), get_words calls split_words_aux with empty accumulator, and select_words_spec filters words by consonant count matching n. The iterative Python implementation of split_words_aux produces the same results as the recursive Coq version for all concrete inputs.
Python mirrors Coq: same vowel set, counts any non-vowel char, splits on spaces ignoring empties, and checks ans equals the filtered words list.
Python mirrors Coq: same vowel set, counts any non-vowel char, splits on spaces ignoring empty segments, and checks ans equals the filtered words list. Concrete outputs match for all inputs.
Python mirrors Coq: same vowel set, counts non-vowel chars, splits on spaces ignoring empties, and filters words by equal counts with preserved order.
